phases.name: Innovators
phases.description: The enthusiasts and inventors. Symbolic AI, expert systems and early machine learning — the technology barely works, but its pioneers build it anyway. Adoption is negligible.
phases.transition: Visionaries arrive as GPU-trained neural nets and statistical ML begin to deliver real, if narrow, results.

phases.name: Early Adopters
phases.description: The visionaries. Deep Blue, then the deep-learning breakthrough (AlexNet, 2012) — narrow superhuman results win believers well ahead of the mainstream. The pre-chasm run-up.
phases.transition: Crossing the chasm (Moore): foundation models make AI useful to pragmatists, not just visionaries.

phases.name: Early Majority
phases.description: The pragmatists — the mainstream begins. Foundation models, ChatGPT and agents carry AI across the chasm into broad use. The steep ascent running up to the logistic inflection at 50.
phases.transition: Passing the 50% inflection: the growth rate peaks and adoption becomes the default.

phases.name: Late Majority
phases.description: The skeptics — they adopt because the holdouts now must. Post-inflection: still growing but decelerating; AI is embedded across the economy out of necessity.
phases.transition: Only laggards remain: the hardest, last cognitive domains automate.

phases.name: Laggards
phases.description: The last holdouts. AI performs ≥95% of economically valuable cognitive tasks at expert-human level; the curve flattens into saturation.
